info.title: Sly API (internal)
info.description: The Agentic Economy Platform — stablecoin payments, agent wallets, multi-protocol commerce, and AI agent orchestration.

post.description: Register an API endpoint that will accept x402 micropayments. Returns a payment address and asset details to advertise in 402 responses.

get.description: List all x402 endpoints registered by your tenant.

409.description: Endpoint has transaction history; add ?force=true to confirm

post.description: A merchant creates a UCP token representing an order. The token is returned to the buyer who exchanges it for settlement via their preferred rail.
Opens an Agentic Commerce Protocol checkout session. The returned client_secret is used to complete payment with the chosen payment method.
A mandate is a signed authorization that lets an agent spend on behalf of an account within defined scope. The returned mandate_jwt can be presented at any AP2-compliant merchant.

get.description: Find agents offering a given skill, ranked by reputation, price, or recency. Public via the non-authed /a2a/marketplace mount; this authed version carries tenant context for personalized ranking.

post.description: Invoke an MCP method (tools/list, tools/call, resources/list, resources/read). Follows the Model Context Protocol JSON-RPC spec.
Creates a person or business account under the authenticated tenant. Accounts start at verification tier 0; use POST /v1/accounts/:id/verify to trigger KYC/KYB.

get.description: Paginated list. Filter by type (person / business), verification status, or free-text search over name + email.

patch.description: Partial update. Only provided fields change; omit a field to leave it as-is. Set `email: null` to clear an email.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the account. Fails if the account has agents, open streams, or a non-zero balance. Detach / drain those first.

post.description: Creates an account, an optional agent, and a wallet in a single atomic call. Returns all generated credentials. Useful for wizards and scripted setups. Sandbox-funded if `initial_funding` is provided.

Move funds between accounts. Same-currency transfers settle instantly; cross-currency transfers require a prior `quote_id` from POST /v1/quotes. Always send an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry.

schema.description: Prevents duplicate transfers on retry; cached for 24h

parameters.description: Prevents duplicate transfers on retry; cached for 24h
parameters.name: idempotency-key

201.description: Transfer created (may still be pending settlement)

403.description: KYA tier, wallet policy, or approval threshold blocked

409.description: Idempotency key reused with different parameters

post.description: Cancels a transfer that hasn't yet been submitted to the settlement rail. Once a transfer is in `processing` or `completed`, cancellation fails — use [/v1/refunds](#tag/Refunds) instead.

409.description: Invalid state transition — transfer already processing or completed

post.description: Register an AI actor under a parent account. The response contains credentials — the `agent_*` token and (if requested) an Ed25519 keypair. Both the token and the private key are shown **once** and not recoverable.

schema.description: Filter to agents currently holding an SSE session

parameters.description: Filter to agents currently holding an SSE session
parameters.name: connected

delete.description: Permanent revocation. All credentials (agent_*, sess_*, Ed25519 keys) are invalidated. Agent wallets are detached but not deleted — funds remain claimable.

get.description: Returns agent's KYA tier caps, the parent account's KYC tier caps, and the effective (minimum-of-both) limits. Plus spend-to-date against each.

post.description: Agent can still authenticate and read data; spending is blocked. Non-destructive — unfreeze via /activate. For full revocation use DELETE /agents/:id or DELETE /agents/:id/auth-keys.
Create a wallet under an account or agent. Network auto-selects based on currency + tenant config if not specified. Stablecoin wallets get an on-chain address; fiat-ledger wallets don't.
Register an on-chain address the customer already controls — Sly tracks it but does not custody funds. Useful for payouts to user-controlled wallets.

delete.description: Fails if the wallet has a non-zero balance. Drain first via /withdraw.

get.description: Point-in-time balance snapshot. `available` = spendable now; `pending` = held by in-flight transfers.

post.description: Creates an internal transfer from the specified source wallet to this wallet. Both wallets must be same currency.
Move funds out — to another Sly wallet (internal) or an on-chain address (external). For large amounts may trigger [compliance hold](/settlement/overview#degraded-modes).
Open a continuous payment flow. `initial_deposit` is locked into the stream escrow; flow_rate drains it per second. Returns the stream with computed `runway_seconds`.
Pauses a stream. Paused streams don't accrue balance but wrapped funds stay locked.
Resumes a stream. Paused streams don't accrue balance but wrapped funds stay locked.
Cancellation transfers accrued balance to recipient and returns unused funds to sender. Stream state becomes `cancelled`.
Add funds to extend runway. Sender wallet is debited; wrapped_balance increases.
Creates a `stream_withdraw` transfer moving accrued recipient_balance into the recipient wallet. Omit amount to withdraw all accrued.
Get a time-bound FX rate quotation for cross-currency transfers. Default TTL ~30 seconds. Use the returned `id` on POST /v1/transfers to lock the rate.

get.description: Informational rate snapshot across all your enabled corridors. NOT a quote — do not use for execution.

post.summary: Lock FX rate for extended window
post.description: Enterprise feature — lock a rate for minutes-to-hours at a higher spread. Returns a quote-like token usable on transfers within the lock window.
Quote a chained conversion (e.g. USD → USDC → BRL via Pix) in a single call. Useful for cross-rail transfers where direct corridors don't exist.

Initiate a refund against a completed transfer. Omit `amount` for full refund. Multiple partial refunds per transfer are supported as long as the cumulative total ≤ original amount. Always send `X-Idempotency-Key`.

409.description: Refund window expired, amount exceeds remaining, or state transition invalid

schema.description: Filter to disputes with response window expiring in <72h

parameters.description: Filter to disputes with response window expiring in <72h
parameters.name: due_soon

409.description: Dispute window expired or duplicate dispute

get.description: Includes full timeline from audit log.

post.description: The counterparty submits their side with counter-evidence. Must be within `response_window_ends_at`; missing the window auto-resolves in the filer's favor.

409.description: Response window expired or dispute already resolved

post.description: Terminal action. If `resolution` is `full_refund` or `partial_refund`, auto-creates a [refund](#tag/Refunds).

cancelMandate.description: If the dispute relates to an AP2 mandate, optionally revoke it

get.description: Counts by status, reason, resolution outcome, and average resolution time. Useful for product/ops dashboards.

post.description: Returns `secret` (whsec_*) shown once — save it to verify signatures. See [signature verification](/sdks/webhook-verification).

get.description: Returns the machine-readable catalog of event types your tenant can subscribe to.
Per-endpoint and tenant-wide delivery counters (delivered, failed, retrying, DLQ).

post.description: Replay events from a time window. Use `only_failed: true` to skip already-delivered events. Replays carry the same `X-Sly-Event-Id`; your dedupe protects against double-processing.

override_url.description: Redirect replays to a different URL — useful when testing a new endpoint against real traffic

get.description: Deliveries that exhausted all retries. Subscribe to `webhook.dlq` for real-time alerts.

delete.description: Permanently removes DLQ entries. Usually done after manual review confirms the events are either truly failed or have been processed manually.

post.description: Returns the plaintext key **once**. Save it to your secrets manager immediately — Sly stores only a hash. Use narrow scopes in production.

get.description: Returns metadata only (prefix is safe to display). The plaintext keys are never retrievable after creation.

delete.description: Permanent. The key is invalidated instantly; cached verifications may persist up to 60s. Use alongside [overlap rotation](/authentication/api-keys#rotate-a-key) for zero-downtime swaps.

post.description: Issues a new key with the same scopes and name, then invalidates the old one. Returns the new plaintext key **once**.

bearerAuth.description: API key (pk_test_* or pk_live_*), JWT session, agent token (agent_*), Ed25519 session (sess_*), or portal token (portal_*).

token.description: JWS-signed token to return to the buyer

initial_funding.description: Sandbox only — funds the wallet from the faucet

quoteId.description: Required for cross-currency transfers; lock FX rate via POST /v1/quotes first

connected.description: True iff an SSE session is currently open

privateKey.description: base64 Ed25519 private key — shown ONCE

generateKeypair.description: If true, returns an Ed25519 keypair (private key shown once)

network.description: Auto-selected based on currency + tenant config if omitted

flow_rate.description: Amount per second, as a decimal string

secret.description: whsec_* — shown ONCE on creation; used for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification

prefix.description: First 12 chars of the key — safe to display
